title: An Asteroid Is About to Pass Closer Than the Moon
script: An asteroid the size of a building is about to skim past Earth, closer than the moon. Astronomers spotted the space rock just weeks ago, tracking it as it races toward us at over twenty thousand miles an hour. It will miss us; but on a cosmic scale, this is a near miss, passing inside the moon's orbit. Rocks this size slip by more often than you'd think, and we don't always see them coming until they're close. If one this big ever did hit, it could flatten a city. Telescopes around the world will watch it streak by. So how many more are out there we haven't spotted?
